risk_title: Security situation delays project implementation in southern Lebanon
risk_summary: Unstable security conditions in Nabatieh and Tyre service zones are impacting data collection and project activities, requiring shift to virtual meetings and remote tools to mitigate risks.
evidence_quote: Given the unstable security situation in Nabatieh and Tyre, the mission recommended that data collection efforts are based on virtual meetings and remote tools where possible to mitigate security risks and ensure continuity of the work.

risk_title: Component 2 implementation jeopardized by security escalation
risk_summary: Security situation in Lebanon has caused slippage in Component 2 implementation, with activities previously put on hold due to escalation, potentially jeopardizing project development objectives.
evidence_quote: Component 2 observed a slippage in implementation due to the security situation, its implementation may be jeopardizing the project development objectives.

risk_title: Stakeholder opposition and threats to Srar Sanitary Landfill project
risk_summary: A landowner's brother operating an adjacent open dump opposes the Srar SLF project and is threatening to damage it if not included in the project. This grievance must be resolved before civil works commence.
evidence_quote: The complainant opposes the SLF because is operates an open dump landfill on the adjacent land plot. He is insisting to be included in the project as well, otherwise he is threatening he will damage the SLF supported by the project. The mission communicated that these competing demands must be resolved by the MoE, Akkar Governor and the Union of Municipalities before any civil work start for the Srar SLF.

risk_title: Conflict-related rubble management activity cannot be completed within project timeline
risk_summary: Management of asbestos-contaminated rubble from the 2023-2024 conflict at the Bakalian site cannot be completed within the project's timeline and budget will be cancelled, with implementation deferred to future operations.
evidence_quote: The mission acknowledged that the management of asbestos-contaminated rubble at the Bakalian site including its safe disposal cannot be completed within the project's timeline and therefore the budget allocated to this activity will be cancelled.

risk_title: Delayed ESIA for Nabatieh SLF due to security issues
risk_summary: Environmental and Social Impact Assessment for Nabatieh sanitary landfill was delayed due to security issues, now under development since February 2025 with expected finalization by August 2025.
evidence_quote: The ESIA for the Nabatieh SLF was delayed due to security issues, however it is currently under development since February 2025 and is expected to be finalized by August 2025.

risk_title: Governance fragility affecting fund transfers and project execution
risk_summary: Withdrawal application from October 2024 faced extensive delays in processing by Ministry of Finance and Ministry of Environment, indicating governance challenges in fund management and project administration.
evidence_quote: The withdrawal application requested by MoE in October 2024 has faced extensive delays in being processed by the Ministry of Finance (MoF) and MoE and is now being actively followed up by MoE to ensure its timely receipt in the project's designated account.

risk_title: Occupational health and safety violations at Karantina facility amid conflict context
risk_summary: Multiple serious OHS violations observed at Karantina Sorting Facility including inadequate PPE, unsecured work areas, and unreported incidents requiring hospitalization. Context of post-conflict reconstruction increases vulnerability.
evidence_quote: Workers were not equipped with appropriate Personal Protective Equipment (PPE), and none were wearing safety shoes... OHS incidents resulting in emergency hospital visits were recorded in the incident log however they were not reported to the World Bank.

risk_title: Procurement delays at UNDP-implemented project due to governance and coordination challenges
risk_summary: Beirut Critical Environmental Recovery project faces continued delays in procurement due to lengthy internal UNDP procedures and external coordination challenges among government stakeholders, resulting in USD 2.2 million budget cancellation.
evidence_quote: The project has faced continued delays in the processing of planned procurement activities. These delays are largely attributed to lengthy internal procedures and approval requirements within UNDP, which have significantly slowed implementation. Additionally, external coordination challenges among key government stakeholders have further compounded these delays.
